Document:
# Definitions and Glossary
## Prisoner of War (POW) POWs are defined as anyone who is a members of a nation's armed forces or in a militia with all of these four distinctions: - Command structure. - Distinctive signs or uniform. - Carrying arms openly. - Following laws of war.
## Commander The leader of the faction, interchangeable with the Acting Commander.
## Adjutant May be reffered to as the XO, is the 2nd in command of the operation.
## Acting Commander The individual acting in place of the Commander holds full command authority and is vested with all powers of the Commander for the duration of their appointment.
## Command Staff Personnel holding senior leadership positions responsible for planning, oversight, and command of operations, including the Commander and subordinate officers.
## Delegated Command The temporary transfer of authority from a superior officer to a subordinate.
## Area of Operations (AO) The designated zone in which operations are being conducted. This is considered anywhere outside of your faction's ship/installation.
## Hostile Personnel Any individual or group demonstrating intent or capability to inflict harm on personnel, civilians or objectives.
## Non-Combatant Individuals not actively participating in hostilities, generally civilians.
## Protected Persons Civilians, medical personnel, surrendering combatants and any individual otherwise safeguarded under the laws of war.
## Lethal Force Force intended or reasonably expected to cause death or serious bodily harm.
## Non-Lethal Force Force intended to incapacitate or compel compliance without causing death. Less-than-lethal weapons that still deal minor amounts of damage such as baton rounds are counted as Non-Lethal force.
## Secure Area Any location defended by your faction that has at least one full line of fortifications or five or more posted guards.
## Personnel All members of the applicable faction.
## Auxiliary Staff/Support Crew Non combat roles that provide logistical, police, medical, or engineering support.
## Flight Crew Pilots and Dropship Crew Chiefs.
## Third-Party Personnel Individuals or groups not affiliated with the primary factions (OPFOR/GOVFOR/CLF) involved in the operation.
## Combat Information Center (CIC) The command and control hub of a ship or installation.
## Special Weapon Any weapon not issued to general members of the faction, usually acquired from the special weapons vendor or ASRS.
## Heavy Weapon Any weapon thats does not fire a standard rifle, pistol, or smg round. Or uses non-standard pistol, rifle, or smg magazines, for example box magazines on an L/HMG. This includes weapons that fire High Explosive munitions like rockets or grenades. This does not include HEAP magazines.
